---
title: "Akashiwo"
subtitle: "Large, unarmored (naked) dinoflagellate, dorsoventrally flattened, with no cellulose thecal plates; a common red-tide former."
categories: [Dinoflagellates]
---

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/build_pages.py — do not edit by hand. Edit meta.yml and re-run the script. -->

**Group:** Dinoflagellates  ·  **Optics:** high-mag, low-mag

## Defining characteristics

A relatively large athecate ("naked", no armour plates) dinoflagellate that is distinctly flattened dorsoventrally, so it looks broad and leaf-like from one side and thin edge-on. The cingulum (girdle) is only modestly displaced, and an apical groove runs over the top of the cell. Cells lack the rigid plate pattern of armored dinoflagellates and can appear brownish from chloroplasts. Akashiwo sanguinea (formerly Gymnodinium sanguineum / splendens) forms red tides. Key features: large size, smooth naked outline, dorsoventral flattening.

## Distinguishing from similar classes

| Similar class | How to tell them apart |
|---|---|
| **gyrodinium** | Both are unarmored, so don't separate them on plates. Gyrodinium's cingulum is strongly displaced into an S-shaped (sigmoid), twisted girdle; Akashiwo is larger, dorsoventrally flattened, and its girdle is only slightly offset. |

## Ideal images

_No images yet — add files to this folder._

## Challenging images

_No images yet — add files to this folder._

## References

- [Akashiwo sanguinea — Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akashiwo_sanguinea)
- [Akashiwo sanguinea — Smithsonian IRL species inventory](https://naturalhistory2.si.edu/smsfp/irlspec/Akashi_sangui.htm)
